description: Shared color palette for blog figure rendering. Both the Node chart renderer (figure-renderer.js) and the Python map renderer (build_section_map.py) read this file so static PNG fallbacks stay visually consistent with the browser's interactive widgets. Update in one place; both paths pick it up. Uses a LIGHT palette because the blog page is light-themed — dark chart backgrounds would jar against the surrounding prose and axis labels would need to flip color between static and hydrated views.
